# react-layered-dialog

> Type-safe, headless dialog/modal management library for React

## What It Does

react-layered-dialog manages dialog state and layering without owning any UI.
You provide the component; the library handles open/close state, z-index stacking,
async resolution, and React subscription.

## Core Concepts

- **DialogStore** — central store holding all dialog entries. Not a React component.
- **DialogsRenderer** — React component that reads the store and renders dialog entries.
- **useDialogController** — hook used *inside* a dialog component to access state and controls.
- **useDialogStore** — hook to subscribe to store snapshots in any React component.
- **createDialogApi** — factory that wraps a store + registry into a typed, named API.

## Installation

```bash
npm install react-layered-dialog
```

## Minimal Setup

```tsx
// lib/dialogs.ts
import { DialogStore, createDialogApi } from 'react-layered-dialog';

const store = new DialogStore();

export const dialog = createDialogApi(store, {
  alert: { component: AlertDialog },                     // sync
  confirm: { component: ConfirmDialog, mode: 'async' },  // async (returns Promise)
});

export { store };

// App.tsx
import { DialogsRenderer } from 'react-layered-dialog';
import { store } from './lib/dialogs';

function App() {
  return (
    <>
      <MyRoutes />
      <DialogsRenderer store={store} />
    </>
  );
}
```

## Opening Dialogs

### Sync (fire and forget)

```ts
// via registry
const handle = dialog.alert({ title: 'Done', message: 'Saved.' });

// via store directly
const handle = store.open(() => <MyDialog />, { id: 'optional-id' });

handle.close();           // isOpen = false  (triggers exit animation)
handle.unmount();         // removes entry from store
handle.update({ message: 'Updated' });
handle.setStatus('loading');
handle.ref.id;            // e.g. "alert-0"
```

### Async (awaitable)

```ts
// via registry (mode: 'async')
const result = await dialog.confirm({ message: 'Delete?' });

// via store directly
const result = await store.openAsync(() => <ConfirmDialog />);

result.ok;      // boolean — what the user chose
result.data;    // optional typed payload from resolve()
result.close();
result.ref.id;
```

> **Important**: if you want to show a loading state *after* the user confirms,
> call `resolve()` without calling `close()`. The dialog stays mounted so you can
> call `result.setStatus('loading')` on the returned handle before closing.
>
> ```ts
> // inside ConfirmDialog
> onConfirm: () => controller.resolve?.({ ok: true }), // do NOT close here
>
> // caller side
> const result = await dialog.confirm(...);
> result.setStatus('loading');
> await doWork();
> result.close();
> ```

## Dialog Component Pattern

```tsx
import { useDialogController } from 'react-layered-dialog';

type Props = { title: string; message: string };

function AlertDialog(props: Props) {
  const { isOpen, zIndex, close, unmount, getProps, status, setStatus } =
    useDialogController<Props>();

  // getProps merges initial props with any store.update() changes
  const { title, message } = getProps(props);

  return (
    // AnimatePresence pattern: close() sets isOpen=false, unmount() removes from DOM
    <AnimatePresence onExitComplete={unmount}>
      {isOpen && (
        <motion.div style={{ zIndex }} exit={{ opacity: 0 }}>
          <h2>{title}</h2>
          <p>{message}</p>
          <button onClick={close}>OK</button>
        </motion.div>
      )}
    </AnimatePresence>
  );
}
```

## Async Dialog Component Pattern

```tsx
type ConfirmProps = { message: string };
type ConfirmData  = { itemId: string };

function ConfirmDialog(props: ConfirmProps) {
  const { isOpen, zIndex, close, unmount, resolve, reject, getProps } =
    useDialogController<ConfirmProps>();

  const { message } = getProps(props);

  return (
    <AnimatePresence onExitComplete={unmount}>
      {isOpen && (
        <motion.div style={{ zIndex }} exit={{ opacity: 0 }}>
          <p>{message}</p>
          <button onClick={() => { resolve?.({ ok: true }); close(); }}>Confirm</button>
          <button onClick={() => { resolve?.({ ok: false }); close(); }}>Cancel</button>
        </motion.div>
      )}
    </AnimatePresence>
  );
}
```

## useDialogController Return Values

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` | Unique dialog ID |
| `isOpen` | `boolean` | Drive animations; false = start exit |
| `zIndex` | `number` | Current z-index, apply to root element |
| `ref` | `DialogRef` | `{ id, componentKey }` |
| `stack` | `DialogStackInfo` | `{ topId, size, index }` |
| `state` | `TProps` | Current store state (from `update()`) |
| `close` | `() => void` | Set isOpen = false |
| `unmount` | `() => void` | Remove from store/DOM |
| `closeAll` | `() => void` | Close all dialogs |
| `unmountAll` | `() => void` | Remove all dialogs |
| `update` | `(updater) => void` | Merge partial state |
| `getProps` | `(base) => merged` | Merge base props with store state |
| `status` | `DialogStatus` | `'idle' \| 'loading' \| 'done' \| 'error'` |
| `setStatus` | `(s) => void` | Set status |
| `resolve` | `(payload) => void` | Async only — fulfill the Promise |
| `reject` | `(reason?) => void` | Async only — reject the Promise |

## DialogStore API

```ts
const store = new DialogStore({ baseZIndex?: number }); // default baseZIndex: 1000

// Open
store.open(renderer, options?)                // returns DialogHandle
store.openAsync(renderer, options?)           // returns Promise<DialogAsyncResult>

// Close / remove
store.close(id?)                              // id omitted = last in stack
store.unmount(id?)
store.closeAll()
store.unmountAll()                            // also auto-rejects pending async dialogs

// State
store.update(id, updaterOrPartial)
store.setStatus(id, status)
store.getStatus(id)
store.isOpen(id)                              // boolean

// React subscription
store.getSnapshot()                           // DialogStoreSnapshot
store.subscribe(listener)                     // returns unsubscribe fn
```

## useDialogStore Hook

Subscribe to store state inside any React component:

```tsx
import { useDialogStore } from 'react-layered-dialog';

function Backdrop() {
  const { entries } = useDialogStore(store);
  const anyOpen = entries.some(e => e.isOpen);
  return anyOpen ? <div className="backdrop" /> : null;
}
```

## createDialogApi

```ts
const dialog = createDialogApi(store, {
  alert:   { component: AlertDialog },                    // sync  → returns DialogHandle
  confirm: { component: ConfirmDialog, mode: 'async' },   // async → returns Promise
});

// IDs are auto-prefixed by registry key: "confirm-0", "confirm-1", ...
dialog.alert({ title: 'Hi' });
const result = await dialog.confirm({ message: 'Sure?' });

// Override ID
dialog.alert({ title: 'Hi' }, { id: 'my-alert' });

// Base store methods are also available on the api object
dialog.store          // the DialogStore instance
dialog.closeAll()
dialog.unmountAll()
```

## Types

```ts
type DialogStatus = 'idle' | 'loading' | 'done' | 'error';

type DialogRef = { id: string; componentKey: string };

type DialogHandle<TProps> = {
  ref: DialogRef;
  close: () => void;
  unmount: () => void;
  update: (updater: Partial<TProps> | ((prev: TProps) => Partial<TProps>)) => void;
  setStatus: (s: DialogStatus) => void;
  getStatus: () => DialogStatus;
  status: DialogStatus;
  zIndex: number;
  getProp: <V>(key: keyof TProps, fallback: V) => V;
  getProps: <T extends Record<string, unknown>>(base: T) => T;
};

type DialogAsyncResult<TProps, TData> = DialogHandle<TProps> & {
  ok: boolean;
  data?: TData;
};

// Convenience controller types for typed annotations inside dialog components
type SyncDialogController<TProps> =
  Omit<DialogControllerContextValue<TProps>, 'resolve' | 'reject'>;

type AsyncDialogController<TProps, TData = unknown> =
  Omit<DialogControllerContextValue<TProps>, 'resolve' | 'reject'> & {
    resolve: (payload: { ok: boolean; data?: TData }) => void;
    reject: (reason?: unknown) => void;
  };
```

## SSR / Next.js

Do **not** create a `DialogStore` at module scope on the server — it is shared across requests.

```tsx
// app/providers.tsx  (Next.js App Router)
'use client';
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { DialogStore, DialogsRenderer } from 'react-layered-dialog';

export function DialogProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [store, setStore] = useState<DialogStore | null>(null);

  useEffect(() => { setStore(new DialogStore()); }, []);

  return (
    <>
      {children}
      {store && <DialogsRenderer store={store} />}
    </>
  );
}
```

## Usage Outside React (Axios Interceptors, etc.)

```ts
// lib/globalDialog.ts
import { DialogStore } from 'react-layered-dialog';

let _store: DialogStore | null = null;

export function getGlobalStore() {
  if (typeof window === 'undefined') return null;  // SSR guard
  return (_store ??= new DialogStore());
}

// axios interceptor
axios.interceptors.response.use(null, (error) => {
  if (error.response?.status === 401) {
    getGlobalStore()?.open(() => <SessionExpiredModal />);
  }
  return Promise.reject(error);
});
```

## Multi-Store

```tsx
const mainStore    = new DialogStore({ baseZIndex: 1000 });
const overlayStore = new DialogStore({ baseZIndex: 9000 });

<DialogsRenderer store={mainStore} />
<DialogsRenderer store={overlayStore} />
```

## Common Mistakes

1. **Calling `close()` and `resolve()` together in async dialogs** — if you need to show loading after confirm, call only `resolve()` first, then close after your async work completes.
2. **Global store in SSR** — use `useEffect` or lazy singleton with `typeof window` guard.
3. **Forgetting `unmount()`** — `close()` only sets `isOpen = false`. The entry stays in the store until `unmount()` is called. Use `onExitComplete={unmount}` in AnimatePresence.
4. **Duplicate IDs** — `store.open()` throws if an entry with the same ID already exists. Use `store.isOpen(id)` to guard.

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-layered-dialog
- GitHub: https://github.com/seunjin/react-layered-dialog
- Docs: https://seunjin.github.io/react-layered-dialog/
